reasoning: The flagged .devcontainer/post-create.sh is a development tooling script not executed during AUR installation. It's irrelevant to package safety as it's never invoked by PKGBUILD functions.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged a development script as unsafe while deepseek skipped source review entirely
coverage_issues: deepseek failed to review source files (skipped tarball)
Both models missed reviewing build/install hooks referenced in PKGBUILD
false_positives: .devcontainer/post-create.sh: Development script misclassified as risk; not executed during AUR build/install
false_negatives: Potential risks in npm build scripts: Neither model reviewed pnpm-related hooks executed during build()
learnings: Improve path resolution: Models must handle extracted source paths correctly
Enforce review priority: PKGBUILD-referenced scripts > build tools > other source
Train models to distinguish development artifacts from build-time execution risks
Implement binary file analysis: Skipping tarballs creates critical coverage gaps
